R Notebook

 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
Error in `dplyr::rename()`:
! Can't rename columns that don't exist.
✖ Column `Sharks Id` doesn't exist.
Run `]8;;x-r-run:rlang::last_trace()rlang::last_trace()]8;;` to see where the error occurred.
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    esr        = `ESR (mm/h)`
  )
Error in `dplyr::rename()`:
! Can't rename columns that don't exist.
✖ Column `ESR (mm/h)` doesn't exist.
Run `]8;;x-r-run:rlang::last_trace()rlang::last_trace()]8;;` to see where the error occurred.
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas%>% 
  filter(!is.na(Shark_Id))

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Antibody_titer is numeric
antibody_datas<- antibody_datas%>%
  mutate(Antibody_titer = as.numeric(Antibody_titer))

# Check for NAs after conversion (in case there were non-numeric values)
sum(is.na(antibody_datas$Antibody_titer))
[1] 0
antibody_datas%>%
  summarise(shapiro_p = shapiro.test(Antibody_titer)$p.value)
library(car)

# Levene's Test for homogeneity of variances across groups
leveneTest(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = antibody_datas)
Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
      Df F value Pr(>F)
group  2  1.1577 0.3213
      59               
library(ggplot2)
 library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

qq_plots_titer <- antibody_datas%>%
  # Convert week to a factor for proper grouping
  mutate(week_factor = factor(week)) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(sample = Antibody_titer)) +
  # Create a separate panel for every unique Groupe/week combination
  facet_grid(Groupe ~ week_factor) +
  # Add the plot points and the normality line
  stat_qq() + 
  stat_qq_line() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots for Antibody_titer: Normality Check by Groupe and Week",
    x = "Theoretical Quantiles (Normal Distribution)",
    y = "Sample Quantiles (Antibody Titer, log2)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(qq_plots_titer)


library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
library(writexl)
Error in library(writexl) : there is no package called ‘writexl’
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
library(writexl)
library(DHARMa)

# --- 0. Data Preparation and Rank Transformation ---

# 1. Apply Rank Transformation to the zero-inflated variable
# CRITICAL FIX: Create the rank column
antibody_datas$Antibody_titer_rank <- rank(antibody_datas$Antibody_titer)

# 2. Ensure factors are set
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas%>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# 3. Clean Group Name
antibody_datas$Groupe[antibody_datas$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Control"


# --- 1. Fit the LMM to Rank-Transformed Data ---

# CRITICAL FIX: Use the rank-transformed variable
lmm_Ab_rank <- lmer(
  Antibody_titer_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = antibody_datas
)

print("--- Summary of Rank-LMM for Antibody Titer ---")
[1] "--- Summary of Rank-LMM for Antibody Titer ---"
summary(lmm_Ab_rank)
Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Satterthwaite's method ['lmerModLmerTest']
Formula: Antibody_titer_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor +      (1 | Shark_Id_factor)
   Data: antibody_datas

REML criterion at convergence: 443.7

Scaled residuals:

fit warnings:
fixed-effect model matrix is rank deficient so dropping 9 columns / coefficients
# ----------------------------
# 2. Residual diagnostics
# ----------------------------

print("\n--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Rank-LMM ---")
[1] "\n--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Rank-LMM ---"
res_Ab_rank <- simulateResiduals(lmm_Ab_rank, n = 1000)

# Plotting residuals (will open a separate plot window)
plot(res_Ab_rank)


testUniformity(res_Ab_rank)

    Asymptotic one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test

data:  simulationOutput$scaledResiduals
D = 0.078065, p-value = 0.8443
alternative hypothesis: two-sided

testDispersion(res_Ab_rank)

    DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs. simulated

data:  simulationOutput
dispersion = 0.8529, p-value = 0.61
alternative hypothesis: two.sided

testZeroInflation(res_Ab_rank)

    DHARMa zero-inflation test via comparison to expected zeros with simulation under H0 = fitted model

data:  simulationOutput
ratioObsSim = NaN, p-value = 1
alternative hypothesis: two.sided

# ----------------------------
# 3. Post-hoc comparisons (Tukey-adjusted)
# ----------------------------

# Use the rank model output
emms_Ab_rank <- emmeans(lmm_Ab_rank, ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor)

# Function to create Excel-friendly pairwise table (Modified for simplicity and clarity)
prepare_emmeans_df <- function(emms_obj) {
  df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emms_obj, adjust = "tukey"))
  
  # Select and rename columns for clarity
  df <- df %>%
    select(contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) %>%
    rename(`P-Value` = p.value)
  
  return(df)
}

Ab_pairs_df <- prepare_emmeans_df(emms_Ab_rank)
print("\n--- Post-hoc Pairwise Comparisons (Ranked Data) ---")
[1] "\n--- Post-hoc Pairwise Comparisons (Ranked Data) ---"
print(Ab_pairs_df)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Export to Excel
# ----------------------------
# CRITICAL FIX: Update filename to reflect Rank-LMM
write_xlsx(Ab_pairs_df, "Antibody_Titer_Rank_Pairwise.xlsx")

print("\nSuccessfully exported: Antibody_Titer_Rank_Pairwise.xlsx")
[1] "\nSuccessfully exported: Antibody_Titer_Rank_Pairwise.xlsx"
library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix)

# Weeks to test
weeks_to_test <- sort(unique(Blood_datas$week))

# Prepare results storage
kw_results <- tibble(
  Week = integer(),
  Statistic = double(),
  df = double(),
  p_value = double()
)

# Loop through weeks
for(wk in weeks_to_test){
  week_data <- Blood_datas %>% filter(week == wk)
  
  kw <- kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = week_data)
  
  kw_results <- kw_results %>% 
    add_row(
      Week = wk,
      Statistic = kw$statistic,
      df = kw$parameter,
      p_value = kw$p.value
    )
}

# Print results
kw_results

# Optional: save to Excel
write_xlsx(kw_results, "Kruskal_Antibody_Weekwise.xlsx")

library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix)

# Kruskal-Wallis test across all weeks
kw_overall <- kruskal_test(Blood_datas, Antibody_titer ~ Groupe)
kw_overall

# Optional: post-hoc pairwise comparisons using Dunn test
pairwise_dunn <- Blood_datas %>%
  dunn_test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
pairwise_dunn

# Optional: save to Excel
library(writexl)
write_xlsx(list(
  KW_Overall = kw_overall,
  Pairwise_Dunn = pairwise_dunn
), "Antibody_Overall_Groups.xlsx")

kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 4))
kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 6))
kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 8))
antibody_datas %>%
  group_by(Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(n = n())
antibody_datas %>%
  filter(Antibody_titer > 10) %>%
  group_by(Groupe) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), 
            min = min(Antibody_titer),
            max = max(Antibody_titer))
levels(antibody_datas$Groupe_factor)
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = ifelse(Groupe == "Contrôle", "Control", Groupe),
    week_num = case_when(
      week == 5 ~ 4,
      week == 7 ~ 6,
      week == 9 ~ 8,
      TRUE ~ week
    ),
    week_factor = factor(week_num, levels = c("4", "6", "8")),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Control", "Summer")),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )
summer_data <- antibody_datas[antibody_datas$Groupe_factor == "Summer", ]
colSums(is.na(summer_data))
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(as.character(week_num), levels = c("4", "6", "8"))
  )
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = ifelse(Groupe == "Contrôle", "Control", Groupe),
    week_num = case_when(
      week == 5 ~ 4,
      week == 7 ~ 6,
      week == 9 ~ 8,
      TRUE ~ week
    ),
    week_factor = factor(week_num, levels = c("4", "6", "8")),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Control", "Summer")),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )
antibody_datas$week_factor <- factor(as.character(antibody_datas$week_num), levels = c("4", "6", "8"))
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  mutate(Antibody_rank = rank(Antibody_titer, ties.method = "average"))

lmm_Ab_rank <- lmer(
  Antibody_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = antibody_datas
)

summary(lmm_Ab_rank)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ----------------------------
# 0) Prepare data & factors
# ----------------------------
antibody_datas <- antibody_datas %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = ifelse(Groupe == "Contrôle", "Control", Groupe),
    week_num = case_when(
      week == 5 ~ 4,
      week == 7 ~ 6,
      week == 9 ~ 8,
      TRUE ~ as.numeric(week)
    ),
    week_factor = factor(as.character(week_num), levels = c("4", "6", "8")),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Control", "Summer")),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Antibody_rank = rank(Antibody_titer, ties.method = "average")
  )

group_colors <- c("Winter" = "#FFF5E6", "Control" = "#FDB888", "Summer" = "#B30000")

# ----------------------------
# 1) Fit LMM
# ----------------------------
lmm_Ab_rank <- lmer(
  Antibody_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = antibody_datas
)

# ----------------------------
# 2) Pairwise comparisons (CLD letters)
# ----------------------------
summary(lmm_Ab_rank)
Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use
  Satterthwaite's method [lmerModLmerTest]
Formula: 
Antibody_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 |  
    Shark_Id_factor)
   Data: antibody_datas

REML criterion at convergence: 418.2

Scaled residuals: 
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-1.76690 -0.57175  0.06407  0.43738  2.02855 

Random effects:
 Groups          Name        Variance Std.Dev.
 Shark_Id_factor (Intercept) 204.2    14.29   
 Residual                    109.0    10.44   
Number of obs: 59, groups:  Shark_Id_factor, 20

Fixed effects:
                                  Estimate
(Intercept)                         33.588
Groupe_factorControl                -5.949
Groupe_factorSummer                 -8.646
week_factor6                        -3.000
week_factor8                        -9.429
Sex_factorMale                       0.596
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor6   10.375
Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6    -0.900
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor8   20.554
Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8    10.273

Groupe_factorSummer                   10.364 25.767

week_factor8                           5.581 32.966

Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6       8.646 32.966
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor8      7.642 32.966
Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8       9.078 33.363
                                  t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept)                         4.307 0.000269

Groupe_factorSummer                -0.834 0.411852

week_factor8                       -1.689 0.100584

Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6   -0.104 0.917727
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor8   2.689 0.011140
Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8    1.132 0.265828

Groupe_factorSummer

Signif. codes:  
0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Correlation of Fixed Effects:

Grp_fctrSmm -0.544  0.470

week_factr8 -0.358  0.304  0.269  0.500

Grp_fctS:_8  0.233 -0.188 -0.397 -0.307 -0.615

Grp_fctS:_6  0.000  0.471              
Grp_fctC:_8  0.000  0.500  0.236       
Grp_fctS:_8 -0.024  0.224  0.476  0.449
print(lmm_Ab_rank)
Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerModLmerTest']
Formula: 
Antibody_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 |  
    Shark_Id_factor)
   Data: antibody_datas
REML criterion at convergence: 418.211
Random effects:
 Groups          Name        Std.Dev.
 Shark_Id_factor (Intercept) 14.29   
 Residual                    10.44   
Number of obs: 59, groups:  Shark_Id_factor, 20
Fixed Effects:
                      (Intercept)  
                           33.588  
             Groupe_factorControl  
                           -5.948  
              Groupe_factorSummer  
                           -8.646  
                     week_factor6  
                           -3.000  
                     week_factor8  
                           -9.429  
                   Sex_factorMale  
                            0.596  
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor6  
                           10.375  
 Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6  
                           -0.900  
Groupe_factorControl:week_factor8  
                           20.554  
 Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8  
                           10.273  
anova(lmm_Ab_rank)
Type III Analysis of Variance Table with Satterthwaite's method
                          Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF
Groupe_factor             134.42  67.212     2
week_factor                 7.12   3.558     2
Sex_factor                  0.79   0.786     1
Groupe_factor:week_factor 879.79 219.948     4
                           DenDF F value Pr(>F)
Groupe_factor             16.049  0.6165 0.5521
week_factor               33.160  0.0326 0.9679
Sex_factor                16.031  0.0072 0.9334
Groupe_factor:week_factor 33.125  2.0175 0.1147
# ----------------------------
# 3) Compute boxplot stats for positioning letters
# ----------------------------
box_stats_rank <- antibody_datas %>%
  group_by(week_factor, Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    ymin   = min(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(Antibody_titer, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    middle = median(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(Antibody_titer, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax   = max(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ----------------------------
# 4) Plot Antibody titer
# ----------------------------
p_Ab_rank <- ggplot(antibody_datas,
                    aes(x = week_factor, y = Antibody_titer, fill = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Groupe_factor),
             shape = 21, size = 2, stroke = 0.5,
             position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors,
                    labels = c("Winter" = "Winter",
                               "Control" = "Static",
                               "Summer" = "Summer")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Winter" = "black",
                                "Control" = "black",
                                "Summer" = "black"),
                     guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Antibody Titer", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 22)
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = "n.s.",
    hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1,
    size = 10
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8")) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

print(p_Ab_rank)

# ----------------------------
# 5) Export
# ----------------------------
ggsave("Figure_antibody_final.png", p_Ab_rank, width = 15, height = 12, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Figure_antibody_final.pdf", p_Ab_rank, width = 15, height = 12)
